title: Mildly Yeasty Greek Pork Chops Gyro
description: A kid-friendly Greek dish featuring pork chops in a yeasty and velvety gyro, made with ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly ingredients.
instructions: In a small bowl, combine yeast and warm water, stirring to dissolve the yeast.
Let the yeast mixture sit for 5 minutes until it becomes frothy.
In a large bowl, whisk together yogurt, lemon juice, olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the yeast mixture to the bowl and stir to combine.
Add the pork chops to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat and cook the pork chops for 5-6 minutes per side.
Warm the pita bread by wrapping it in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the gyro by slicing the cooked pork chops and placing them in the pita bread with tomato, red onion, and a dollop of the yeast sauce.
